channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Mina the Hollower Sells Over 300k Copies in 3 Days
item.content:encoded:
Move over Mickey Mouse, there's a new hot mouse in the world as Mina the Hollower sales have crossed 300k copies in under 3 days.

After clearing over 55k copies in its first morning on Steam, the little mouse that could has continued to sell with Yacht Club Games president Sean Velasco revealing the sales figures to Jason Schrier. The sales compare favorably to their great Shovel Knight, which took a week to sell 75k copies across Steam, 3DS, and Wii U in 2014, and sold over 2.75m by 2019. Velasco has said that he'd be disappointed if Mina the Hollower sales don't reach at least a million.

Mina the Hollower began as one of two projects after Yacht Club Games after Shovel Knight King of Cards, alongside a 3D Shovel Knight project that was eventually put on the backburner to focus on Mina. Mina The Hollower is a classic example of a game growing much bigger than expected in development, with the original side project taking 6 years to develop and an estimated budget of over $5 million.

Reception of Mina the Hollower has been nothing short of fantastic, and encouraging to Velasco as well. Here at TechRaptor it captured one of our rare 10/10s by Austin Suther who called it "one of the greatest retro throwbacks of all time", and that is far from an isolated opinion. On OpenCritic Mina the Hollower has a score of 92, which is the second highest of the year only to visual novel Schrödinger's Call, beating out games like Pokemon Pokopia, and Forza Horizon 6.

With this success, the concern that Mina the Hollower could be the studio's swan song appear to be premature. While understandable given how the studio had invested so much in the title, it seems that it will be profitable in the near future.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don
